codeReview.summary: The commit substantially improves RPC runtime diagnostics and correctly adds focused coverage for the five specified failure modes (empty stdout, invalid JSON, non-object result, error envelope, non-zero exit). However, the implementation does not fully satisfy the explicit requirement that all runtime errors include method context: two remaining NodeProviderRuntimeError paths still omit the RPC method.

issues.description: Missing-entrypoint error omits RPC method context. Requirement states all errors must include the called method (e.g., list_definitions/execute/materialize). Current message includes plugin_id and path only.
Invalid definitions-shape error omits method context ('list_definitions'). This violates the feature requirement that all runtime errors include method context.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared baseline command in services.yaml still combines Python + frontend tests despite mission-documented pre-existing Vitest failures in db/node_provider_plugins, causing repeated procedural deviations in backend-only features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml -> commands.test uses 'uv run pytest -x -q --tb=short && bun run test --run'; mission AGENTS.md 'Known Pre-Existing Issues' documents 7 expected Vitest failures in db/node_provider_plugins/covalt-n8n-nodes; handoff for this feature records deviation from baseline for this reason.

summary: FAIL — core RPC error handling improvements are strong, but two in-scope runtime error paths still do not include method context, which is an explicit feature requirement.
